cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Andrew could have gone to the clothing store. Let me start by listing all the information given and see where there are gaps in his schedule. First, Andrew woke up at 5am. Then, William saw him playing tennis from 5am to 6am. So that's the first hour after waking up. From 6am to 9am, Jessica saw him buying lunch at the deli. Then, from 9am to 11am, Tiffany saw him taking photos near the Leaning Tower of Pisa. That's interesting because the Leaning Tower of Pisa is in Italy, so maybe Andrew is traveling? Anyway, moving on. From 11am to 5pm, John saw Andrew waiting at the train station. That's a long time, from 11am until 5pm. Then, Sarah saw him reading at the library from 6pm to 9pm. The clothing store was closed after 9pm, so Andrew had to go before that. Now, let's map out his timeline: - 5am - 6am: Playing tennis - 6am - 9am: Buying lunch at deli - 9am - 11am: Taking photos at Pisa - 11am - 5pm: Waiting at train station - 6pm - 9pm: Reading at library So let's check for any gaps between these activities. From 5am to 6am: occupied. Then 6am to 9am: occupied. Then 9am to 11am: occupied. Then 11am to 5pm: occupied. Then 5pm to 6pm: what's happening here? The last activity before 6pm is waiting at the train station until 5pm. Then the next activity is reading at the library from 6pm. So between 5pm and 6pm, there's a gap. That's the only time not accounted for. So Andrew could have gone to the clothing store during that 5pm to 6pm window. The clothing store was closed after 9pm, so going at 5-6pm is fine. Let me check the options again. Options are: (A) 9am to 11am – he was taking photos (B) 6pm to 9pm – he was reading at the library (C) 11am to 5pm – he was at the train
